title: Moral Skepticism and Epistemic Pressure
description: Introduce skeptical pressure on moral judgments and test coherence.

steps.text: You face the classic trolley decision to save five by diverting the trolley.

steps.prompt: What is your judgment about diverting?

steps.text: Test whether the constraint against killing supports your judgment.
Moral skepticism questions whether we have moral knowledge or justification.

steps.notes: Assess coherence while allowing skeptical pressure to lower confidence.

steps.prompt: What evidence or method would strengthen your confidence in this judgment?
